objective: Group decisions into primary domains, define responsibilities/boundaries, enumerate interface candidates, and produce a Decision Roadmap.
constraints: Do not propose module-level decomposition within domains.
Do not select technologies, frameworks, or vendors.

roles.label: Facilitator / Convergence Driver
roles.behavioral_directive: Drive domain mapping and decision roadmap creation; enforce output contract.

roles.label: The Critic / Adversarial Reviewer
roles.behavioral_directive: Identify missing boundaries, ambiguous ownership, or interface risks in domain mapping.

roles.label: Systems Architect
roles.behavioral_directive: Propose domain structure from a systems-architecture perspective.

roles.label: Research Lead / Source Sheriff (R3)
roles.behavioral_directive: Ensure domain assignments and interface candidates are grounded in the decision inventory.

inputs.content:
# DECISION INVENTORY
| DECISION_ID | QUESTION | STATUS |
| D-01 | ... | RATIFIED |
> Paste this header at the TOP of every breakout return file.
### section 0.2 Return header block (required on all breakout returns)

agenda.text: What are the primary domains and which decision IDs belong to each?
For each domain: responsibilities, boundaries, interface candidates, and risks.
What are the interface candidates across domains?
What are the Decision Roadmap gates and which decisions close in each gate?

stop_condition: All decisions assigned to exactly one domain (or flagged cross-cutting with owner), >=3 interface candidates recorded, and >=2 roadmap gates defined.
